title: Oscar Bait Reborn: 'Hustlers' Cashes In, 'The Goldfinch' Flops Hard, and 'Jojo Rabbit' Hops into the Best Picture Race | The Oscars Show
format: A post-festival Oscar check-in: Jojo Rabbit takes the TIFF Audience Award, Hustlers overperforms, The Goldfinch bombs, then a too-early Best Picture board and Matt Tyrnauer on his Roy Cohn doc.

segments.heading: The Oscar Race After the Festivals
segments.note: Jojo Rabbit wins the TIFF Audience Award

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: Taika Waititi's 'anti-hate satire' took Toronto's Audience Award despite a 49 Metacritic. It's the most divisive winner of a prize that almost always predicts a Best Picture nominee.

films.title: Green Book
films.note: A recent Audience Award winner Sean cites as the template for a crowd-pleaser that rides Toronto straight to the Oscars.

films.title: Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri
films.note: The 2017 Toronto winner, part of the run of divisive audience picks.

films.title: La La Land
films.note: Won the Audience Award 'for a reason': the kind of movie Toronto crowds reward.

films.title: Where Do We Go Now?
films.note: Nadine Labaki's film, the last Audience Award winner (2011) that missed a Best Picture nomination.

films.title: Thor: Ragnarok
films.note: Cited as proof Waititi is a real filmmaker before Jojo.

films.title: Hunt for the Wilderpeople
films.note: The Waititi film Sean names as evidence of his range.

films.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
films.note: The only recent case of a critically pummeled film riding straight onto the Oscar conveyor belt, the precedent Jojo would follow.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: A Toronto runner-up and, with Parasite, one of the two best-reviewed films of the festival season; a near-lock for Best Picture.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: The other runner-up; the wave of positivity around it is unusual for a subtitled genre film.

films.title: If Beale Street Could Talk
films.note: A prior-year runner-up that landed a Best Picture nomination.

films.title: Roma
films.note: The other 2018 runner-up that went on to multiple nominations and wins.

films.title: Moonlight
films.note: Emerged from Telluride and won; the open question is whether it was the exception or the new order.

films.title: The Shape of Water
films.note: Grouped with Three Billboards and Green Book as the possible 'new reality' of Oscar winners.

segments.heading: Stock Up: 'Hustlers'
segments.note: a $33M opening and a J.Lo awards case

films.title: Hustlers
films.note: Lorene Scafaria's adaptation of Jessica Pressler's New York magazine story opened to $33M, huge for a starless-IP mid-budget drama; 65% of the audience was women. Sean reads it as a friendship rom-com in crime-movie clothes, carried by Jennifer Lopez's Ramona.

films.title: Goodfellas
films.note: Sean's running bit: 'Hustlers 2' is just Goodfellas with an all-male stripper cast, him as Henry Hill's son.

films.title: Magic Mike XXL
films.note: Amanda's guess for what the all-male 'Hustlers 2' would actually be.

films.title: Argo
films.note: Cited as the rare case of magazine journalism making a good movie, a comparison for Hustlers' adapted-screenplay odds.

segments.heading: Stock Down: 'The Goldfinch'
segments.note: the biggest bomb of the year

films.title: The Goldfinch
films.note: John Crowley's adaptation of Donna Tartt's Pulitzer novel made $2.5M on 2,500 screens. Shot by Roger Deakins, but too episodic and inert; both hosts land on 'they had an idea and it just didn't work.'

films.title: Where'd You Go, Bernadette
films.note: The 'good effort, admirable failure' literary adaptation Amanda hoped The Goldfinch would at least match.

films.title: The Souvenir
films.note: Sean's counter-example: a film that can under-share and still work, which a plot-heavy book like this can't.

films.title: Brooklyn
films.note: The earlier John Crowley film that makes The Goldfinch's failure more surprising.

films.title: Anna Karenina
films.note: Joe Wright's adaptation, offered as proof a thousand-page book can be adapted with a real vision of scale.

films.title: It
films.note: The first chapter works better than The Goldfinch at the same flip-back-and-forth structure.

films.title: It Chapter Two
films.note: The other half of the Stephen King adaptation whose childhood/adulthood cross-cutting Sean compares to The Goldfinch's.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: The only 'lock' on the board that has actually been released; the Sharon Tate role is the quiet flip side of J.Lo's Ramona.

films.title: Joker
films.note: The diciest of the contenders: split reception out of Venice and Toronto, already lost to the culture war, but tipped to be 'the Venom of this season.'

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Unseen, heading to the New York Film Festival in two weeks, but a Scorsese movie. Advertised during the last Oscars with the 'in theaters and on Netflix' spot.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: One of the contenders Sean feels strongest about out of the festivals.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: In the 'green / slow down' tier: unseen, Christmas-dated, a Greta Gerwig adaptation trading on old Oscar timing.
The other green pick: Sam Mendes and Roger Deakins doing a WWI one-take war movie. If voters fell over themselves for Birdman, what happens here?

films.title: A Hidden Life
films.note: Terrence Malick, on the long doubtful list.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: The 'Instagram indie movie,' marketed straight at Amanda; some prognosticators (Scott Feinberg) have it in the mix, though Sean is dubious.

films.title: The Peanut Butter Falcon
films.note: The 'Facebook indie movie,' a $15M word-of-mouth hit with a similar profile to The Farewell but less cultural cache.

films.title: The Last Black Man in San Francisco
films.note: Grouped with The Farewell as an indie that did decent business this year.

films.title: The Lighthouse
films.note: Sean's 'TikTok movie' / old-parchment-and-quill pick; Amanda counters that Hustlers is the actual TikTok movie.

films.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood
films.note: Warm reaction, maybe; one of only two Sony releases in the mix.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: Destin Daniel Cretton's legal drama with Michael B. Jordan, Brie Larson and Jamie Foxx: 'respectful,' which isn't what you want out of a festival.

films.title: Us
films.note: Came out this year; Amanda liked it but neither expects it in the awards mix.

films.title: The Report
films.note: A respectful festival reception since Sundance; the Annette Bening vehicle in the supporting-actress conversation.

films.title: The Lion King
films.note: 'A movie with a bad story,' as discussed previously on the show.

films.title: Ad Astra
films.note: On the doubtful list: maybe too meditative for the Academy; teased with a James Gray interview later that week.

films.title: Waves
films.note: Strong early reviews, negative pans out of TIFF; could be a box-office hit like The Farewell more than an Oscar movie.

films.title: Dolemite Is My Name
films.note: Warmly received; Eddie Murphy will get a Golden Globe nod and campaign on SNL, but it's not a Best Picture thing.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: 'It'd be amazing' if it reached the Best Picture race. Sean doesn't see it happening.

films.title: Booksmart
films.note: 'Remember Booksmart?' Fondly named, not seen as happening.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The running Thanos bit: 'Thanos will be nominated for Best Supporting Thanos.'

films.title: The Good Liar
films.note: Unseen Warner Bros. drama with Ian McKellen and Judi Dench; 'feels like an Amanda movie.'

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: Extremely well received at TIFF; probably not an Oscar movie, but everyone's excited.

films.title: Gemini Man
films.note: The Ang Lee / Will Smith movie coming in five weeks.

films.title: Judy
films.note: Renee Zellweger a lock for Best Actress; the movie is not.

films.title: Dark Waters
films.note: Todd Haynes legal drama that might threaten Just Mercy's acknowledgement, on the 'unknown' tier.

films.title: Queen & Slim
films.note: Melina Matsoukas' film from a Lena Waithe / James Frey story, with Daniel Kaluuya.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: Named for Adam Driver: 'that guy just might be Pacino.'

films.title: Cats
films.note: 'It's only September' and everyone keeps making Amanda consider Cats; floated for a full musical episode.

films.title: Bombshell
films.note: The Fox News film with Charlize Theron, 'new Oscar bait,' the self-identity way in.

films.title: The Banker
films.note: Apple's George Nolfi true story with Samuel L. Jackson, Anthony Mackie and Nicholas Hoult: the test of whether Apple TV+ can platform an Oscar movie.

segments.heading: Matt Tyrnauer on 'Where's My Roy Cohn?'

films.title: Where's My Roy Cohn?
films.note: Tyrnauer's documentary on the McCarthy counsel turned Trump mentor, built as a film noir: the story of a closeted gay power broker and 'the whole point,' the hypocrisy of the Lavender Scare.

films.title: Studio 54
films.note: His prior doc, where the Cohn idea was born: Cohn was the club's lawyer and a denizen. On Netflix.

films.title: Scotty and the Secret History of Hollywood
films.note: Mostly cinéma vérité; the Scotty Bowers gas-station-brothel story he spent years chasing and defends as fully cross-checked truth.

films.title: Valentino: The Last Emperor
films.note: His first film, also a vérité portrait.

films.title: Grey Gardens
films.note: The Maysles landmark he names as a major influence, and a hero of American direct cinema.

films.title: Hustle
films.note: An obscure Robert Aldrich color noir with Burt Reynolds and Catherine Deneuve: the brown-and-Bushmills palette he borrowed for the Cohn film.

films.title: Kiss Me Deadly
films.note: The Aldrich noir that ends in a nuclear explosion, Tyrnauer's 'five gold stars' example of noir nihilism.

films.title: White Heat
films.note: The Cagney film that ends with the refinery going up in flames: the conflagration he ties to Cohn and Trump.

films.title: Green for Danger
films.note: Sidney Gilliat's British noir, a Criterion title, and Tyrnauer's answer for the last great thing he's seen.

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

The Last Black Man in San Francisco.title: The Last Black Man in San Francisco

A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker

Scotty and the Secret History of Hollywo.title: Scotty and the Secret History of Hollywood
